description_long: This is a freestanding, cast-iron wood-burning stove with a rectangular body and a prominent cylindrical chimney pipe extending vertically from the top. The stove features a flat, overhanging top surface and four short, outward-curved legs that provide stability. The front face includes a rectangular door with a large, slightly arched window and a metallic handle, as well as a smaller knob above the door, likely for airflow control. The door is bordered by metallic accents, and the overall finish is matte black, giving it a robust, utilitarian appearance. The sides and back are plain, with no visible openings or controls, emphasizing the stove's functional design. The chimney pipe is centrally positioned and dominates the vertical profile of the stove.
description: A matte black, cast-iron wood-burning stove with a large front door, metallic handle, and a tall cylindrical chimney pipe.
description_view_0: Side view showing the plain, solid side panel and the chimney pipe extending vertically from the top.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main door with a window, metallic handle, control knob, and the central chimney pipe.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the side panel and chimney pipe visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain, flat back panel and the chimney pipe, with no visible controls or openings.
attribute_control_panel_design: No dedicated control panel is visible; a single round knob above the door likely serves as an airflow or damper control. There are no digital displays, touchscreens, or labeled buttons present.
attribute_door_handle_type: The door handle is a horizontally mounted, cylindrical metallic bar with a rounded end, protruding from the right side of the door. It appears to be made of metal with a polished or brushed finish, designed for easy gripping.
attribute_window_presence: present; a large, rectangular window with a slightly arched top is set into the front door, occupying most of the door's surface.
